compatibilityLabel: React DOM 19.2.6 resource hint API oracle

generation.method: checked runtime inventory plus exact npm tarballs extracted into a temporary node_modules tree
generation.probeIsolation: one Node child process per React DOM resource-hint scenario and mode

probeModes.reason: Default Node development captures public warnings, return values, and private dispatcher normalization for the root React DOM facade.
Default Node production captures the warning-free production root facade while preserving resource dispatcher argument normalization.
React Server Components development captures the narrowed root facade and its development resource-hint diagnostics.
React Server Components production captures the narrowed warning-free root resource-hint facade.

scenarios.reason: Records descriptors and function lengths for the six public React DOM resource hint APIs.
Calls valid resource hints with React DOM's default no-op dispatcher to prove return values and absence of warnings without a renderer-installed dispatcher.
Exercises invalid and edge arguments so development warning calls can be separated from production's silent normalization behavior.
Temporarily replaces ReactDOMSharedInternals.d to observe normalized arguments crossing the private resource dispatcher boundary. This is implementation evidence, not a public API claim.
Temporarily removes private dispatcher methods to record the thrown shape when internals are corrupted. Public no-renderer behavior is covered by the default dispatcher scenario.

args.value: ReactDOM.prefetchDNS(): Expected the `href` argument (first) to be a non-empty string but encountered %s instead.
ReactDOM.prefetchDNS(): Expected the `href` argument (first) to be a non-empty string but encountered %s instead.

ReactDOM.prefetchDNS(): Expected only one argument, `href`, but encountered %s as a second argument instead. This argument is reserved for future options and is currently disallowed. It looks like the you are attempting to set a crossOrigin property for this DNS lookup hint. Browsers do not perform DNS queries using CORS and setting this attribute on the resource hint has no effect. Try calling ReactDOM.prefetchDNS() with just a single string argument, `href`.
ReactDOM.preconnect(): Expected the `href` argument (first) to be a non-empty string but encountered %s instead.
ReactDOM.preconnect(): Expected the `options` argument (second) to be an object but encountered %s instead. The only supported option at this time is `crossOrigin` which accepts a string.
ReactDOM.preconnect(): Expected the `crossOrigin` option (second argument) to be a string but encountered %s instead. Try removing this option or passing a string value instead.
ReactDOM.preload(): Expected two arguments, a non-empty `href` string and an `options` object with an `as` property valid for a `<link rel="preload" as="..." />` tag.%s
The `href` argument encountered was an empty string.
ReactDOM.preload(): Expected two arguments, a non-empty `href` string and an `options` object with an `as` property valid for a `<link rel="preload" as="..." />` tag.%s
ReactDOM.preload(): Expected two arguments, a non-empty `href` string and an `options` object with an `as` property valid for a `<link rel="preload" as="..." />` tag.%s
The `as` option encountered was an empty string.
ReactDOM.preloadModule(): Expected two arguments, a non-empty `href` string and, optionally, an `options` object with an `as` property valid for a `<link rel="modulepreload" as="..." />` tag.%s
The `href` argument encountered was an empty string. The `options` argument encountered was something with type "number".
ReactDOM.preloadModule(): Expected two arguments, a non-empty `href` string and, optionally, an `options` object with an `as` property valid for a `<link rel="modulepreload" as="..." />` tag.%s
ReactDOM.preinit(): Expected the `href` argument (first) to be a non-empty string but encountered %s instead.
ReactDOM.preinit(): Expected the `options` argument (second) to be an object with an `as` property describing the type of resource to be preinitialized but encountered %s instead.

ReactDOM.preinit(): Expected the `as` property in the `options` argument (second) to contain a valid value describing the type of resource to be preinitialized but encountered %s instead. Valid values for `as` are "style" and "script".
ReactDOM.preinitModule(): Expected up to two arguments, a non-empty `href` string and, optionally, an `options` object with a valid `as` property.%s
The `href` argument encountered was an empty string.
ReactDOM.preinitModule(): Expected up to two arguments, a non-empty `href` string and, optionally, an `options` object with a valid `as` property.%s
The `options` argument encountered was something with type "string".
ReactDOM.preinitModule(): Expected up to two arguments, a non-empty `href` string and, optionally, an `options` object with a valid `as` property.%s
ReactDOM.prefetchDNS(): Expected the `href` argument (first) to be a non-empty string but encountered %s instead.

ReactDOM.prefetchDNS(): Expected only one argument, `href`, but encountered %s as a second argument instead. This argument is reserved for future options and is currently disallowed. It looks like the you are attempting to set a crossOrigin property for this DNS lookup hint. Browsers do not perform DNS queries using CORS and setting this attribute on the resource hint has no effect. Try calling ReactDOM.prefetchDNS() with just a single string argument, `href`.
ReactDOM.preload(): Expected two arguments, a non-empty `href` string and an `options` object with an `as` property valid for a `<link rel="preload" as="..." />` tag.%s
The `as` option encountered was an empty string.
ReactDOM.preinitModule(): Expected up to two arguments, a non-empty `href` string and, optionally, an `options` object with a valid `as` property.%s

value.caveat: These probes mutate ReactDOM private internals and are recorded only to document failure shape when the private dispatcher is absent or corrupted.
These probes mutate ReactDOM private internals and are recorded only to document failure shape when the private dispatcher is absent or corrupted.

privateInternalsPolicy.publicCompatibilitySurface: Function descriptors, warning calls, thrown values, and return values from ReactDOM public resource hint APIs.
privateInternalsPolicy.privateEvidenceOnly: ReactDOMSharedInternals.d method names and normalized dispatcher arguments are recorded only to identify implementation root causes. They are not public API compatibility promises.

intentionalGaps.reason: This oracle records pinned React DOM package behavior only; Fast React React DOM implementation is owned by separate workers and remains unclaimed here.
Argument normalization is observed by mutating React DOM private internals. The public contract is limited to call outcomes, warnings, throws, and return values.
The oracle does not create DOM nodes, render roots, run Fizz, assert inserted preload/link tags, or assert response headers.
